title: 以下、進めて。本番環境で動作するか確認せよ。 • 解決しました。Hybrid Failover の即 fail 文言は AGENTS.md で recovery matrix 前提に修正し、run- kernel-council-review.sh には実際の recovery retry を追加しました。最初の council 判定が落ちて も、1 回だけ force_copilot_continuity + gemini/xai backfill + metered override で再試行し、それ でもだめなときだけ fail します。あわせて test-shadow-continui...
summary_text: **Findings (severity order)** 1. **High: label gate loosening can bypass implementation governance for manual dispatch** - In context resolution, manual dispatch inputs can force implementation intent regardless labels (`IMPLEMENT_REQUEST_INPUT` / `IMPLEMENT_CONFIRMED_INPUT` overwrite label-derived values): [resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L275](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L275), [resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L279](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L279). - Today this is contained by `should_run=false` when required labels are missing: [resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L319](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L319). - If you loosen that gate for “explicit manual dispatch”, `codex-implement` path can become reachable on unlabeled issues once Tutti approves: [fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L842](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L842). 2. **High: canary-only bypass semantics can be accidentally broken** - Current exception is deliberately narrow: only `workflow_dispatch` + canary issue + dispatch run id marks `canary_dispatch_owned=true`: [resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L314](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L314). - That exception only relaxes missing `tutti`, not generic label discipline: [resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L319](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L319). - Broad manual-dispatch loosening would collapse this boundary and increase false-positive execution on non-canary issues. 3. **Medium: non-task issues may trigger external side effects** - Existing tests assume skipped issues do not call translation LLM or patch issue content when `should_run=false`: [test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L300](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L300), [test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L307](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L307), [test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L312](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L312). - Loosening gate increases risk of unintended comments/API calls on plain issues. **Test coverage gaps (important)** - No explicit regression test for `workflow_dispatch` with `implement_request=true`, `implement_confirmed=true`, `vote_command=true`, but **without** `fugue-task/tutti` labels, asserting `should_run=false` and `skip_reason=missing-required-labels`. - No explicit test proving canary bypass remains the *only* bypass for missing `tutti` in `workflow_dispatch`. - No end-to-end caller-level test asserting `codex-implement` remains skipped when `ctx.should_run=false` even with manual implement inputs true. **What I ran (local-only)** - `bash tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh` → `37/37 passed` - `bash tests/test-vote-handoff-simulation.sh` → `3/3 passed` - `bash tests/test-kernel-canary-plan.sh` → failed on run-name expectation drift (`issue #... dispatch ...` format check), which is adjacent risk for canary correlation tests. No code changes were made.
